description_long: This sculpture depicts three standing figures on a rectangular base, rendered in a style reminiscent of ancient Egyptian art. The central figure wears a tall, elaborate headdress and a kilt, flanked by two other figures: one with a human head and the other with an animal head, likely representing deities. The figures are carved in high relief, with the two outer figures slightly angled toward the central figure, their arms positioned as if supporting or presenting him. The rear of the sculpture features a vertical slab with hieroglyphic inscriptions, adding to the authenticity and detail. The surface is a weathered, stone-like material with a matte, slightly rough finish, and the overall coloration is a uniform brownish-gray. The base is solid and rectangular, providing stability and a sense of monumentality.
description: A detailed, brownish-gray stone sculpture of three ancient Egyptian figures, with the central figure flanked by a human-headed and an animal-headed figure, all standing on a rectangular base.
description_view_0: Frontal view showing all three figures clearly, with the central figure's headdress and the contrasting heads of the flanking figures most visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the sculpture and the alignment of the figures, with the animal-headed figure in the foreground.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the vertical slab with hieroglyphic inscriptions and the backs of the figures, emphasizing the relief carving.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the human-headed figure in the foreground and the arrangement of the figures from behind.
attribute_location_context: No visible context; background is isolated and does not indicate a specific setting.
